workflow.name: Generate AI ecommerce ads from product page and images with Claude and NanoBanana
workflow.description:
## Generate AI Ecommerce Ads from Product Page and Images with Claude + NanoBanana

### 👥 Who is this for?

This workflow is designed for **ecommerce brands, marketers, agencies, and content teams** who want to generate static product ads faster from minimal product input.

It works especially well if you already have:

- a **product page URL**
- a **logo**
- a **product image**
- a need to turn that into a more structured ecommerce ad concept and final creative without building everything manually

### 🧩 What problem is this workflow solving? / Use case

Creating decent ecommerce ad creatives usually takes more than just prompting an image model.

You need to:

- understand what the product actually is
- extract the real use cases and customer needs
- identify the most important product features and benefits
- pull proof points, pricing, or offer context from the page
- decide what visual direction makes sense for the product
- then generate the final ad

This workflow solves that by automating the full process from **product page + brand assets → product insights → ad concept → generated image**.

### ⚙️ What this workflow does

- Collects a **product URL**, **logo**, and **product image** through a form
- Analyzes the uploaded product image with Claude to understand what kind of product visual it is
- Fetches the product page
- Extracts and cleans product page text into one usable source
- Builds structured product insights such as:
  - **product name**
  - **product summary**
  - **product category**
  - **customer group**
  - **use cases**
  - **problems / needs**
  - **key product features**
  - **key benefits**
  - **proof / trust signals**
  - **offer / pricing**
  - **brand voice**
- Creates an ecommerce ad concept with Claude
- Generates a static ecommerce ad creative with NanoBanana through OpenRouter
- Converts the output into a file and uploads it to Google Drive

### 🔌 Setup

Connect your accounts:

- **Anthropic API** for product insight extraction and ad concept generation
- **OpenRouter** for image analysis and final image generation
- **Google Drive** if you want to store the final output

Set your credentials in the respective nodes.

Make sure your form accepts:

- **.jpg**
- **.png**
- **.webp**

If you do not want file export, disable the **Upload file** node.

### 🛠️ How to customize this workflow to your needs

**Product analysis:**  
Adjust the product insight prompt if you want different fields, such as ingredients, materials, objections, bundle logic, or audience segments.

**Ad concept style:**  
Edit the concept generation prompt to control tone, structure, and creative direction.

**Visual output:**  
Update the image generation prompt to make outputs more minimal, more premium, more editorial, more offer-led, or more product-shot focused.

**Copy structure:**  
Change the allowed copy structures if you want more offer-first, testimonial-first, or badge-led ecommerce ads.

**Export flow:**  
Replace Google Drive with your own storage, CMS, or downstream creative workflow.

### 🔍 How it works

The workflow starts with a form submission containing a product URL, logo, and optional product image. The uploaded assets are processed first: the logo is prepared for generation, while the product image is analyzed to understand whether it is a product shot, packaging, illustration, object, or another kind of asset.

Next, the workflow fetches the product page and converts it into readable text. That content is then cleaned and turned into one usable source. Claude uses it to build structured product insights, including product summary, category, customer group, problems or needs, use cases, features, benefits, trust signals, pricing or offer, and brand voice.

Based on that, Claude creates one ecommerce ad concept with the copy structure, main text, optional supporting text, badges or microcopy, CTA, visual direction, layout direction, and style direction.

Finally, the concept and uploaded assets are passed to the image model to generate the final ecommerce ad creative, which can then be exported automatically.

### ✅ Result

With this workflow, you go from **product page + assets → product insights → ad concept → generated ecommerce creative** in one flow, with much less manual prompting and much more structure.

This is also close to the idea behind **Blumpo**: better ads come from better context first, not just better prompting.

workflow.name: Generate AI Ecommerce Ads from Website and Images with Claude + NanoBanana

parameters.content:
## 1. Product page + asset preparation

**Double click** to edit me.

### What happens here

- The workflow starts with a simple form:
  - **Product URL**
  - **Logo**
  - **Product photo**

- Uploaded assets are separated and prepared
- The **logo** is converted for later use in image generation
- The **product photo** is analyzed to understand what kind of product visual it is
- The product page is fetched and converted into readable text
- That text is cleaned into one usable source for analysis

### Why this matters

This step builds the raw input for the workflow:
- product page context
- brand asset
- product visual asset

Without this layer, the ad would be much more generic.

### Output

- cleaned product page text
- processed logo
- processed product image
- short description of the uploaded product asset

## Setup before running

### Required credentials

- **Anthropic account**  
Used for website/product analysis and ad concept creation

- **OpenRouter account**  
Used for product image analysis and final image generation

- **Google Drive account**  
Used only if you want the final ad uploaded automatically

### Form inputs required

- **Product URL** — required
- **Logo** — required
- **Product photo** — optional, but recommended

### Important checks

- Make sure the uploaded files are **.jpg, .png, or .webp**
- Check that your OpenRouter model names are valid
- If you do not want export, disable the **Upload file** node

parameters.text:
=Identify Language of the dommain:
Input domain:

{{ $('Base inputs').item.json.Website }}
- Identify the top-level domain (TLD), including multi-part endings like .com.pl or .co.uk.

- If the TLD corresponds to a specific country, output that country’s dominant language.

- If the TLD is generic or not tied to a country (e.g., .com, .co, .io, .ai, .net, .org, .app, .dev, .tech), DEFAULT to English.

- If unsure or ambiguous, DEFAULT to English. 

Return only the language as a plain string.
Example: "English"
No JSON. No arrays. No extra text.

=You are an ecommerce product analyst.

Your task is to extract structured information strictly from the provided ecommerce website or product page text.

You must maximize the amount of useful information extracted, but:

Do not invent facts.
Do not guess or rely on prior knowledge.
Only return elements that are directly supported by explicit text or strongly implied within the same paragraph or section.
If any element cannot be found, return "Unknown" or [].

Use short, clear phrasing.
Never include marketing fluff — only what the text clearly states.

WEBSITE / PRODUCT PAGE TEXT
{{ $json.json_safe }}

TASKS

Product / Brand Name
Extract the product name if clearly visible in the text. Prefer product titles, headings, hero sections, product descriptions, or structured product information. Remove domain suffixes (.com, .io, etc.). If both brand and product name appear, include the most relevant product name first and brand second if useful. If only brand is visible, return the brand name. If unsure → "Unknown". Use the website URL only as a weak signal when the name is otherwise unclear.

Product Summary
1–2 neutral, factual sentences describing what the product is and what it does, based ONLY on the text.

Product Category
Short classification of the product type, max 4 words.
Examples: skincare product, fitness equipment, kitchen accessory, pet product, apparel, supplement, home decor.
If unclear → "Unknown".

Customer Group
Who the product is for. Extract exactly as worded when possible. If multiple groups appear, list them separated by commas. If unclear → "Unknown".

Use Cases
List the main situations, needs, or occasions where the product is used.

Problems / Needs
List the core customer pain points, needs, or desires described or clearly implied.

Solution
Short, factual explanation of how the product addresses the problems or needs.

Key Product Features
Extract main functional or physical attributes.
Examples: material, ingredients, size, color, design, compatibility, included items, mechanism, construction, formulation, care instructions.
Do not turn benefits into features.

Key Benefits / Differentiators
Extract value outcomes explicitly stated.
Examples: saves time, improves comfort, reduces mess, supports hydration, easier storage, better fit, longer-lasting, travel-friendly.

Proof / Trust Signals
Extract explicit trust elements if present.
Examples: reviews, ratings, guarantees, certifications, awards, clinical tests, warranty, free returns, shipping claims, before/after claims, customer testimonials.
If none → [].

Offer / Pricing
Extract price, discount, bundle, subscription, free shipping, trial, payment plan, or urgency claims if clearly stated.
If none → "Unknown".

Brand Voice & Tone
Describe the tone based only on the text.
Examples: premium, playful, clinical, minimalist, friendly, bold, luxury, practical, eco-conscious.
If unclear → "Unknown".

Business Type
Short classification of the business type, max 3 words.
Use: Ecommerce.

---

After extracting all fields, write a ~250-word ecommerce marketing brief synthesizing ONLY the extracted information.

Rules for the brief:
- ~250 words.
- No fluff.
- Very dense and insight-heavy.
- Explain the customer’s world, their frustrations, desires, and purchase motivations.
- Show how the product fits the need → solution → benefit → purchase reason arc.
- Mention concrete features, benefits, proof, and offer details only when extracted.
- Maintain the extracted tone.
- No made-up facts.

---

OUTPUT FORMAT (Valid JSON Only)

{
  "product_name": "",
  "product_summary": "",
  "product_category": "",
  "customer_group": "",
  "use_cases": [],
  "problems_needs": [],
  "solution": "",
  "key_product_features": [],
  "key_benefits": [],
  "proof_trust_signals": [],
  "offer_pricing": "",
  "brand_voice": "",
  "marketing_brief": "",
  "business_type": "Ecommerce"
}

Return ONLY this JSON object. No explanations, no markdown, no extra text.

=You are a senior ecommerce marketing strategist, creative director, and conceptual advertising designer known for bold, memorable, conversion-focused product campaigns.

Your task is to create one complete ecommerce advertisement concept that communicates clear product value through strong product presentation, buyer motivation, and playful or unexpected creative expression when appropriate.

All reasoning must rely strictly on the provided inputs.
Do not introduce external assumptions, examples, statistics, claims, use cases, or predefined metaphors.

--------------------------------
LANGUAGE

Create all ad text in:
{{ $json.output.language || "English" }}

--------------------------------
PRIMARY CONTEXT

Product / Brand Name:
{{ $json.output.product_name }}

Product Summary:
{{ $json.output.product_summary }}

Product Category:
{{ $json.output.product_category }}

Customer Group:
{{ $json.output.customer_group }}

Use Cases:
{{ $json.output.use_cases }}

Problems / Needs:
{{ $json.output.problems_needs }}

Key Benefits:
{{ $json.output.key_benefits }}

Key Product Features:
{{ $json.output.key_product_features }}

Proof / Trust Signals:
{{ $json.output.proof_trust_signals }}

Offer / Pricing:
{{ $json.output.offer_pricing }}

Brand Voice:
{{ $json.output.brand_voice }}

Marketing Brief:
{{ $json.output.marketing_brief }}

--------------------------------
PRODUCT ASSET / PRODUCT DESCRIPTION

{{ $('Merge').item.json.Product_description }}

--------------------------------
ASSET INTERPRETATION

First, analyze the provided product asset or product description.

Determine:
- what type of product visual it represents: physical product, packaging, lifestyle image, product detail, ingredient/material close-up, bundle, before/after, icon, abstract graphic, or other
- what concrete product feature, use case, benefit, or buyer desire it can support
- how the product should influence the ad composition

The product must be visually central.
Do not make the product decorative or secondary.

If the asset shows a physical ecommerce product:
- keep the product recognizable and desirable
- do not distort, obscure, crop heavily, or redesign the product
- the ad concept must be built around making the product feel valuable, useful, and easy to understand

--------------------------------
CORE CREATIVE REQUIREMENT

The advertisement must:
- communicate why someone should want or buy the product
- make the product benefit clear quickly
- connect the product to a real need, desire, use case, or outcome from the inputs
- remain believable and grounded in the provided text
- use playful, unexpected, or bold creative expression only when it strengthens the buying reason

Ecommerce ads do not always need a classic headline + subheadline structure.
Choose the copy structure that best fits the product, offer, and creative idea.

Allowed ad copy structures:
- headline + short support line + CTA
- punchy product claim + CTA
- benefit-led headline + feature chips
- problem line + solution line + CTA
- offer-led headline + product benefit + CTA
- testimonial-style line + CTA, only if testimonials are provided
- comparison-style line, only if comparison is clearly supported by inputs
- simple product label + benefit badges + CTA

Do not force a subheadline if a shorter ecommerce layout would be stronger.

--------------------------------
PART 1 — COPY CREATION

Create exactly one ecommerce copy concept.

The concept must include:

1. copy_structure
Choose one:
- "headline_support_cta"
- "claim_cta"
- "benefit_chips_cta"
- "problem_solution_cta"
- "offer_benefit_cta"
- "product_label_badges_cta"
- "testimonial_cta"

2. primary_text
The main visible text of the ad.
Maximum 7 words.
Must be clear, specific, and product-relevant.

3. secondary_text
Optional supporting text.
Maximum 12 words.
Use only if it improves clarity.
If not needed, return "".

4. microcopy_elements
Return 0–3 short elements such as feature chips, benefit badges, offer labels, or product-use labels.
Each element maximum 4 words.
Only include elements supported by the inputs.

5. cta_text
Maximum 3 words.
Short, natural, action-oriented, and ecommerce-appropriate.

COPY RULES:
- use concrete product value, not generic brand language
- make the buying reason clear
- keep the copy visually scannable
- avoid long explanatory sentences
- avoid unsupported numbers, guarantees, reviews, discounts, or claims
- avoid vague phrases like: unlock, empower, seamless, game-changing, life-changing, premium quality, must-have
- do not mention the customer group unless the website text does
- no unusual spelling
- plural “s” must be lowercase

--------------------------------
PART 2 — VISUAL STRUCTURE

CREATIVE IDEA

Describe:
- the product-led creative angle
- the buyer need, desire, or use case it highlights
- any playful or unexpected twist, if useful
- why the idea remains credible for ecommerce

MAIN VISUAL

Describe:
- how the product appears as the central focal point
- the scene, setting, object arrangement, or product display
- how benefits, features, use cases, or offer details appear visually
- how to keep the product recognizable and desirable

LAYOUT

Define:
- the relationship between product, primary text, optional secondary text, microcopy elements, and CTA
- whitespace, balance, and first-glance clarity
- whether the layout should feel product-shot, lifestyle, editorial, comparison, offer-led, or badge-led

STYLE DIRECTION

Specify:
- modern ecommerce ad aesthetic
- clean product-focused composition
- controlled color usage
- polished lighting, depth, or flat graphic precision
- paid-media-ready finish
- tone aligned with the extracted brand voice

--------------------------------
OUTPUT FORMAT

Return only valid JSON:

{
  "copy_structure": "",
  "primary_text": "",
  "secondary_text": "",
  "microcopy_elements": [],
  "cta_text": "",
  "creative_idea": "",
  "main_visual": "",
  "layout_description": "",
  "style_direction": ""
}

Return only the JSON object. No markdown, no explanations, no extra text.

parameters.content:
## 2. Product insights + ad generation

**Double click** to edit me.

### What happens here

- Claude analyzes the product page text and extracts structured ecommerce insights such as:

- Then it creates one ad concept with:
  - primary text
  - optional secondary text
  - microcopy / badges
  - CTA
  - creative idea
  - main visual direction
  - layout direction
  - style direction

- Finally, the image model generates the ad and can export it as a file

### Why this matters

This is where raw product-page information becomes:
1. **product understanding**
2. **creative direction**
3. **final ecommerce ad output**

### Output

- structured product insights
- ecommerce ad concept
- generated static ad
- optional exported file

## What this workflow does

This workflow turns a **product URL, logo, and product image** into a ready ecommerce ad creative.

It reads the product page, extracts the product context, analyzes the uploaded image, builds a product-focused ad concept with Claude, and then generates the final static ad with NanoBanana through OpenRouter.

This is useful when you want more structured, product-aware ecommerce ads instead of generic AI outputs.

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.
